spec_sections.title: CONCRETE FORMING AND ACCESSORIES
spec_sections.work_description:
PART 1 - GENERAL 
1.1 
SUMMARY 
A. 
Section Includes: 
1. 
Form-facing material for cast-in-place concrete. 
2. 
Shoring, bracing, and anchoring. 
1.2 
PREINSTALLATION MEETINGS 
A. 
Preinstallation Conference: Conduct conference at Project site. 
1.3 
ACTION SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Product Data: For each of the following: 
1. 
Exposed surface form-facing material. 
2. 
Waterstops. 
B. 
Sustainable Design Submittals:  
1. 
Recycled content: For each product. 
2. 
Environmental Product Declaration: For each product. 
3. 
Health Product Declaration: For each product. 
4. 
Sourcing of Raw Materials: Corporate sustainability report for each manufacturer. 
5. 
Laboratory Test Reports: For liquid floor treatments and curing and sealing 
compounds, indicating compliance with requirements for low-emitting materials. 
C. 
Shop Drawings: Prepared by, and signed and sealed by, a qualified professional engineer 
responsible for their preparation, detailing fabrication, assembly, and support of forms. 
1. 
For exposed vertical concrete walls, indicate dimensions and form tie locations. 
2. 
Indicate dimension and locations of construction and movement joints required to 
construct the structure in accordance with ACI 301. 
a. 
Location of construction joints is subject to approval of the Architect. 
3. 
Indicate location of waterstops.

1.4 
INFORMATIONAL SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Field quality-control reports. 
B. 
Minutes of preinstallation conference.
spec_sections.execution_requirements:
PART 3 - EXECUTION 
3.1 
INSTALLATION OF FORMWORK 
A. 
Comply with ACI 301. 
B. 
Construct formwork, so concrete members and structures are of size, shape, alignment, 
elevation, and position indicated, within tolerance limits of ACI 117 and to comply with 
the Surface Finish designations specified in Section 033000 "Cast-In-Place Concrete" for 
as-cast finishes. 
C. 
Limit concrete surface irregularities as follows: 
1. 
Surface Finish-1.0: ACI 117 Class D, 1 inch. 
2. 
Surface Finish-2.0: ACI 117 Class B, 1/4 inch. 
3. 
Surface Finish-3.0: ACI 117 Class A, 1/8 inch. 
D. 
Construct forms tight enough to prevent loss of concrete mortar. 
1. 
Minimize joints. 
2. 
Exposed Concrete: Symmetrically align joints in forms. 
E. 
Construct removable forms for easy removal without hammering or prying against 
concrete surfaces. 
1. 
Provide crush or wrecking plates where stripping may damage cast-concrete 
surfaces. 
2. 
Provide top forms for inclined surfaces steeper than 1.5 horizontal to 1 vertical. 
3. 
Install keyways, reglets, recesses, and other accessories, for easy removal. 
F. 
Do not use rust-stained, steel, form-facing material. 
G. 
Set edge forms, bulkheads, and intermediate screed strips for slabs to achieve required 
elevations and slopes in finished concrete surfaces. 
1. 
Provide and secure units to support screed strips. 
2. 
Use strike-off templates or compacting-type screeds.

H. 
Provide temporary openings for cleanouts and inspection ports where interior area of

spec_sections.title: CONCRETE REINFORCING
spec_sections.work_description:
PART 1 - GENERAL 
1.1 
SUMMARY 
A. 
Section Includes: 
1. 
Steel reinforcement bars. 
2. 
Welded-wire reinforcement. 
3. 
Steel fiber reinforcement. 
4. 
Twisted Steel Micro Rebar. 
1.2 
PREINSTALLATION MEETINGS 
A. 
Preinstallation Conference: Conduct conference at Project site. 
1.3 
ACTION SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Product Data: For the following: 
1. 
Each type of steel reinforcement. 
2. 
Bar supports. 
3. 
Mechanical splice couplers. 
4. 
Steel fiber / Twisted Steel Micro Rebar (TSMR) reinforcement manufacturer’s 
standard product description and dosage and mixing instructions.  
B. 
Sustainable Design Submittals: 
1. 
Submit Environmental Product Declaration (EPD): 
a. 
Independent third-party verified. 
b. 
Global Warming Potential (GWP) measured in kgCO2e/kg, tracking 
construction materials stages A1-A3 embodied carbon emissions. Indicate 
inclusion or exclusion of stage A4 emissions. 
2. 
Maximum Allowable GWP: 
a. 
Embodied carbon emissions in stages A1-A3 for fabricated reinforcement 
products must not exceed 0.650 kilograms of CO2 equivalent per kilogram 
of reinforcement (kgCO2e/kg). 
3. 
Product Data: For recycled content, indicating postconsumer and preconsumer 
recycled content and cost. 
4. 
Health Product Declaration: For each product. 
5. 
Sourcing of Raw Materials: Corporate sustainability report for each manufacturer. 
C. 
Shop Drawings: Comply with ACI SP-066:

1. 
Include placing drawings that detail fabrication, bending, and placement. 
2. 
Include bar sizes,
spec_sections.execution_requirements:
PART 3 - EXECUTION 
3.1 
PREPARATION 
A. 
Protection of In-Place Conditions: 
1. 
Do not cut or puncture vapor retarder. 
2. 
Repair damage and reseal vapor retarder before placing concrete. 
B. 
Clean reinforcement of loose rust and mill scale, earth, ice, and other foreign materials 
that reduce bond to concrete. 
3.2 
INSTALLATION OF STEEL REINFORCEMENT 
A. 
Comply with CRSI's "Manual of Standard Practice" for placing and supporting 
reinforcement. 
B. 
Accurately position, support, and secure reinforcement against displacement. 
1. 
Locate and support reinforcement with bar supports to maintain minimum concrete 
cover. 
2. 
Do not tack weld crossing reinforcing bars. 
C. 
Preserve clearance between bars of not less than 1 inch, not less than one bar diameter, 
or not less than 1-1/3 times size of large aggregate, whichever is greater. 
D. 
Provide concrete coverage in accordance with ACI 318. 
E. 
Set wire ties with ends directed into concrete, not toward exposed concrete surfaces. 
F. 
Splices: Lap splices as indicated on Drawings. 
1. 
Bars indicated to be continuous, and all vertical bars shall be lapped not less than 
36 bar diameters at splices, or 24 inches, whichever is greater. 
2. 
Stagger splices in accordance with ACI 318. 
G. 
Install welded-wire reinforcement in longest practicable lengths. 
1. 
Support welded-wire reinforcement in accordance with CRSI "Manual of Standard 
Practice." 
a. 
For reinforcement less than W4.0 or D4.0, continuous support spacing sh

gap_report.summary: Analysis of 2 spec sections via llm. Found 13 gaps: 11 critical, 2 moderate, 0 watch.

items.title: No Division 03 30 00 Cast-in-Place Concrete section is present
items.description: No Division 03 30 00 Cast-in-Place Concrete section is present. Forming (03 10 00) and Reinforcing (03 20 00) are specified but the actual concrete placement, mix design, curing, finishing, and testing requirements are entirely absent. This is the core work product of the concrete division and without it there are no performance requirements, compressive strength specifications, admixture controls, or placement procedures. Contractors will price to their own assumptions, creating significant exposure to disputes and change orders.
items.recommendation: Obtain or issue Division 03 30 00 Cast-in-Place Concrete immediately. Confirm mix designs, compressive strengths (f'c), slump requirements, curing methods, and special inspection requirements before finalising bid. Do not bid without this section.

items.title: Division 31 Earthwork is entirely absent
items.description: Division 31 Earthwork is entirely absent. Any concrete foundation or slab-on-grade work implied by Division 03 sections requires excavation, grading, compaction, and subgrade preparation. Without a Division 31 spec, scope of earthwork, compaction standards, bearing capacity requirements, and disposal of excess material are undefined. This is a critical cost driver that could easily exceed $100K on a commercial project.
items.recommendation: Confirm whether earthwork is in scope for this contract. If so, obtain Division 31 specifications including subgrade preparation, compaction testing requirements, and import/export soil allowances. Clarify via RFI before bid.

items.title: Division 33 Site Utilities is entirely absent
items.description: Division 33 Site Utilities is entirely absent. Concrete construction almost always requires coordination with underground utilities (drainage, water, sewer, electrical conduit sleeves). Without a Division 33 spec, it is unclear who is responsible for underground utility installation, stub-outs, and coordination with the concrete contractor for cast-in sleeves and blockouts.
items.recommendation: Determine whether site utilities are in scope. If yes, issue Division 33 specifications. If utilities are by owner or a separate prime, document this clearly in Division 01 and confirm interface points with the concrete contractor.

items.title: Division 01 General Requirements is entirely absent
items.description: Division 01 General Requirements is entirely absent. There are no specifications covering project coordination, submittals, RFI procedures, schedule requirements, temporary facilities, quality control, special inspections, or closeout requirements. This creates ambiguity about project administration responsibilities and could lead to disputes over who bears costs for items like concrete testing, special inspections (ACI), and mock-ups.
items.recommendation: Obtain Division 01 General Requirements before bidding. At minimum, confirm special inspection requirements for concrete (per IBC Chapter 17), submittal turnaround times, schedule constraints, and temporary facility responsibilities. Issue RFI if not provided.

items.title: Division 00 Procurement and Contracting Requirements is absent
items.description: Division 00 Procurement and Contracting Requirements is absent. Without bid forms, contract terms, bonding requirements, insurance requirements, and general conditions, the contractor cannot properly assess risk, markup, and bonding costs. This is foundational to any bid.
items.recommendation: Confirm that Division 00 documents exist and have been issued with the bid package. If not, request immediately. Do not submit a bid without reviewing contract terms, liquidated damages clauses, retainage, and payment terms.

items.title: Concrete forming (03 10 00) implies embedded items such as anchor bolts, cast-in-place inserts, sleeves, blockouts, and conduit sleeves for other trades
items.description: Concrete forming (03 10 00) implies embedded items such as anchor bolts, cast-in-place inserts, sleeves, blockouts, and conduit sleeves for other trades. No specification section addresses who furnishes and installs these embedded items, nor are there any MEP or structural steel divisions present to claim this scope. This is a classic coordination gap that generates change orders.
items.recommendation: Issue an RFI or addendum clarifying responsibility for all cast-in-place embedded items including anchor bolts, pipe sleeves, conduit sleeves, and blockouts. Confirm whether the concrete contractor or respective trade contractors are responsible for furnishing and setting embedded items prior to concrete placement.

items.title: Concrete Reinforcing (03 20 00) implies shop drawings, bar lists, and rebar detailing by a licensed detailer, as well as special inspection of reinforcing placement per IBC
items.description: Concrete Reinforcing (03 20 00) implies shop drawings, bar lists, and rebar detailing by a licensed detailer, as well as special inspection of reinforcing placement per IBC. No Division 01 submittal process is defined and no special inspection program is referenced anywhere in the spec. Omission of special inspections can result in failed inspections, required demolition, or code compliance issues.
items.recommendation: Confirm that a Special Inspection Program (SIP) has been prepared by the Engineer of Record per IBC Chapter 17. Ensure the cost of the special inspection agency (concrete, rebar, and soils) is included in the bid. Clarify whether the owner or contractor retains the special inspection agency.

items.title: Concrete forming and placement implies concrete mix design submittals, cylinder testing, and third-party materials testing
items.description: Concrete forming and placement implies concrete mix design submittals, cylinder testing, and third-party materials testing. No testing and inspection specification (typically 01 45 00 or 03 30 00) is present. Without defined testing frequency and responsibility, the contractor may not budget for required cylinder breaks, slump tests, and air content tests, or may assume the owner provides this service when they do not.
items.recommendation: Clarify in writing who is responsible for concrete materials testing and special inspections, the required testing frequency (ACI 301/318 minimums), and who pays for retesting if failures occur. Include an allowance in the bid if responsibility is unclear.

items.title: Division 03 forming and reinforcing sections imply a structural foundation and/or elevated concrete system, yet no Division 05 Structural Steel or Division 05 Metal Fabrications section is present
items.description: Division 03 forming and reinforcing sections imply a structural foundation and/or elevated concrete system, yet no Division 05 Structural Steel or Division 05 Metal Fabrications section is present. Most commercial concrete structures include embedded plates, anchor rods, column base plates, or miscellaneous metals. If structural steel or metal fabrications are required, the absence of Division 05 creates an unpriced scope gap.
items.recommendation: Review structural drawings to determine if structural steel, metal decking, or miscellaneous metals are required. If so, confirm Division 05 specifications will be issued. Include a clarification or exclusion in the bid if Division 05 scope cannot be confirmed.

items.title: With only two concrete-related spec sections present and no other divisions, it is unclear whether this is a concrete-only subcontract package or a full general contract with missing specifications
items.description: With only two concrete-related spec sections present and no other divisions, it is unclear whether this is a concrete-only subcontract package or a full general contract with missing specifications. The apparent building type, full scope, and project delivery method are undefined. Bidding without understanding the full project scope creates significant risk of scope gaps and omissions.
items.recommendation: Before finalising any bid, request the complete project specification table of contents, the full set of construction drawings, and the project description from the owner or design team. Confirm the intended scope of this bid package and whether additional specification sections will be issued.

items.title: Concrete slab and foundation work implies vapor retarder, underslab insulation, and granular fill below slabs-on-grade
items.description: Concrete slab and foundation work implies vapor retarder, underslab insulation, and granular fill below slabs-on-grade. No Division 07 Thermal and Moisture Protection section is present. Omission of vapor retarder specification is common and can result in disputes over who supplies and installs this material, and to what ASTM standard.
items.recommendation: Confirm whether a vapor retarder below slab-on-grade is required by the drawings or energy code. If so, request a Division 07 81 00 or 03 30 00 subsection addressing vapor retarder material, lapping, and installation requirements. Include an allowance if unresolved.

items.title: Concrete forming implies the need for shoring and reshoring on multi-level structures, as well as temporary bracing of vertical formwork
items.description: Concrete forming implies the need for shoring and reshoring on multi-level structures, as well as temporary bracing of vertical formwork. No specification section addresses shoring design, engineer-of-record approval of shoring plans, or reshoring requirements. On multi-story concrete construction this is a significant safety and cost item.
items.recommendation: Confirm whether the project involves elevated concrete slabs or multi-story construction. If so, request clarification on shoring and reshoring requirements, including whether a licensed engineer must design and stamp the shoring system. Include shoring engineering costs in the bid.

items.title: No Division 32 Exterior Improvements section is present
items.description: No Division 32 Exterior Improvements section is present. Concrete construction typically interfaces with flatwork, curbs, sidewalks, and paving. If any site concrete is within the contractor's scope, the absence of Division 32 leaves this work unspecified and potentially unpriced.
items.recommendation: Confirm whether site flatwork, curbs, sidewalks, or paving are included in this contract scope. If yes, request Division 32 specifications. If excluded, document the exclusion explicitly in the bid.

takeoff_items.description: Concrete formwork - exposed vertical concrete walls (form-facing material, Surface Finish-2.0 or 3.0)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit wall area given; estimated based on typical mid-size commercial building with cast-in-place walls. Adjust to actual structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Concrete formwork - slabs on grade and elevated slabs (edge forms, bulkheads, screed strips)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit slab area stated; estimated based on typical commercial building footprint. Adjust to structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Concrete formwork - inclined surfaces requiring top forms (slope steeper than 1.5H:1V)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires top forms for inclined surfaces steeper than 1.5:1; area estimated as minor portion of total formwork.

takeoff_items.description: Waterstops - at construction joints in concrete walls and slabs
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires waterstops at construction joints; quantity estimated based on typical below-grade wall and slab joint layout for a commercial building.

takeoff_items.description: Shoring and bracing for concrete formwork
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires shoring, bracing, and anchoring per ACI 301; no explicit quantity given, treated as lump sum scope item.

takeoff_items.description: Crush/wrecking plates at form stripping locations
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires crush or wrecking plates where stripping may damage cast surfaces; count estimated based on typical wall and column form layout.

takeoff_items.description: Temporary cleanout and inspection port openings in formwork
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires temporary openings for cleanouts and inspection ports; count estimated based on typical wall panel layout.

takeoff_items.description: Steel reinforcement bars (deformed rebar, ASTM A615 or A706) - all concrete elements
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit rebar quantity stated; estimated at approximately 5 LB/SF of slab for an 8,000 SF building footprint plus walls and footings. Adjust to structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Welded-wire reinforcement (WWR) - slabs on grade and elevated slabs
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec includes WWR as a section item; area estimated to match slab area assumption. Adjust to structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Steel fiber reinforcement - concrete mix additive
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec lists steel fiber reinforcement as a section item with dosage per manufacturer instructions; no explicit volume or weight given. Quantity depends on concrete volume and dosage rate specified on drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Twisted Steel Micro Rebar (TSMR) - concrete mix additive
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec lists TSMR as a section item with dosage per manufacturer instructions; no explicit quantity given. Quantity depends on concrete volume and dosage rate specified on drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Bar supports (chairs, bolsters) for steel reinforcement
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires bar supports to maintain minimum concrete cover per CRSI; estimated at approximately 1 support per 10 SF of reinforced slab area.

takeoff_items.description: Mechanical splice couplers for reinforcement bars
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec lists mechanical splice couplers as a product data submittal item; count estimated based on typical vertical wall and column bar continuity requirements.

takeoff_items.description: Vapor retarder repair and resealing at punctures/damage prior to concrete placement
takeoff_items.notes: source=specified | Spec explicitly requires repair and resealing of vapor retarder damage before placing concrete; treated as lump sum allowance.

takeoff_items.description: Rebar lap splices - minimum 36 bar diameters or 24 inches at continuous and vertical bars
takeoff_items.notes: source=specified | Spec explicitly defines lap splice length requirements per ACI 318; included as a specified execution requirement. Actual splice count and length to be taken from structural drawings.

exclusions: Overtime and shift premiums unless specifically noted
Permits and fees by Owner unless noted
Testing and inspection services by Owner
Furniture, fixtures, and equipment (FF&E) unless noted
Security and temporary fencing beyond standard requirements
assumptions: Work performed during normal business hours (7AM-3:30PM, M-F)
Site access and staging area provided by Owner
Adequate power and water available on site
No soil remediation or environmental issues
All dimensions verified from construction documents
Subcontractor pricing based on competitive bid market
Material pricing valid for 30 days from estimate date
Quantities based on plan takeoff; field verification recommended
